titleStmt.title: Letter from Micheal Garry to Alexander Richard Barlas, 19 April 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Micheal Garry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Micheal Garry (b.1883), master of the workhouse in Killadysert, County Clare to Alexander Richard Barlas (b.1862). Barlas was the secretary for the Local Government Board. In this Garry is apologizing on behalf of one of his insane work house inmates who has been sending unwanted mail to the king and members of the Royal family.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8784/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
WorkHouse
Killadysert, 19th April, 1916. To
The Secretary,
Local Government board,
Ireland Sir, In reply to your letter dated 18th April 1916,
No.18.276 : 1916 I beg to state that since Mr D'Alton,
Local Government inspector instructed me re Patrick
Hastings an insane inmate of this Workhouse, I have
been taking every precaution to prevent this man
writing and posting letters to His Majesty, The King, or
the members of the Royal Family, and I sincerely regret
any annoyance caused His Majesty in the matter. I am ,Sir, Yours Faithfully, M. Garry,
Master.
